skillName: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°
misconceptionSummary: Students do not understand why angles on a straight line total 180° — they apply the rule mechanically without grasping the half-turn concept.

visuals.ruleText: A straight line represents a half turn — exactly 180° of rotation
visuals.subText: Any angles that together fill that half turn must add to 180°

steps.narration: A full turn is three hundred and sixty degrees. A straight line is exactly half of that — one hundred and eighty degrees. Any angles that together exactly fill the space on one side of that straight line must sum to one hundred and eighty.

lines.text: Angles on one side of the line fill that 180°

steps.narration: Full turn is three hundred and sixty. Half turn is one hundred and eighty. A straight line is a half turn. Any number of angles arranged on one side of a straight line, sharing the same point, together fill exactly one hundred and eighty degrees.

lines.text: Angles on one side only — not both sides
Both sides together would give 360° (full turn)

steps.narration: The rule applies to angles on one side of the line only. Angles on both sides together would give three hundred and sixty — a full turn. The one-hundred-and-eighty-degree rule is specifically for the angles on a single side.

visuals.label: Angles on one side of a straight line fill a half turn — they must sum to 180°

steps.narration: One hundred and eighty degrees — a half turn. The rule is not arbitrary: a straight line is a half rotation, and any angles that subdivide it must add up to that same half turn.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: treating the 180° rule as a fact to memorise with no reason. A straight line is a half turn (180°). Any angles filling one side of it must sum to that half turn — that is where the rule comes from.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The rule comes from rotation: a straight line is a half turn, which is 180°. Angles on one side of it together fill exactly that half turn. The rule has a geometric reason, not just a number to remember.
